brief_summary: Inline glyph strip immediately after the 'The vocabulary settled' Callout. Four side-by-side panels divided by quiet verticals, each carrying a small acoustic-envelope sketch + a serif italic label + a sans subtitle drawn from the Callout verbatim: 'thock' (broad low crest with coral wash and focal dot), 'clack' (tall narrow spike with a faint secondary ring), 'marble' (symmetric mid-rounded envelope with mirrored inner curve), 'popcorn' (scattered warm-grey dots over a dashed broken envelope, no rhythm). Provides a visual anchor for the four words readers will reuse.
data_sources: apps/web/src/content/articles/acoustic-spec-rise.mdx — Callout 'The vocabulary settled' (the four words and their definitions are quoted directly from the article body)
